name: bench-cowork
description: BenchAGI agent fleet + skills for Claude Code. Tier D of the harness ladder — no OpenClaw install required, talks to benchagi.com for canon + billing. 0.4.1: exposes the triage-mail skill in the Claude Code plugin manifest. 0.4.0: adds bench-mail stdio MCP for multi-account Gmail triage. Customers connect Gmail through apps/web Settings → Integrations → Gmail (Bench Mail); the MCP server reads tokens from users/{uid}/communicationConnections (encrypted with ENCRYPTION_KEY). Tools: list_accounts, search_threads, get_thread, create_draft, list_labels, create_label, apply_label — every tool takes an explicit account_email. v1 does NOT request gmail.send. Cycle 6: 7 agents + 8 skills + 4 MCP servers + /bench-login auth + Amendment-10 pre-commit hook. 0.3.5: doc fix — /wiki-capture command frontmatter now references `wiki_draft` (member-auth, single-entry) instead of `wiki_ingest` (cowork-auth, bulk); the SKILL.md and MCP manifest already pointed at wiki_draft since 0.3.1, the command description was the last stale reference. No tool shape changes; wiki_search and wiki_canon_read unchanged. 0.3.4: /bench-login skill now resolves the caller's tenant ID via GET /api/me/identity and caches it as bench_instance_id in the local config; web sidebar avatar links to /app/account. 0.3.3: bench-wiki.wiki_ingest now points at the cowork-authed POST /cowork/wiki/ingest route and hard-shards writes to users/{uid}/wikiEntries/{slug} so Tier D users can bulk-sync a local vault without needing a super-admin key. Entries land as drafts; admin review promotes to platform canon. The prior 0.3.2 super-admin /wiki/ingest route is untouched — it still exists for the OpenClaw daemon. 0.3.2: wiki_draft no longer accepts `instanceId` in the payload — the server derives tenant from auth.instanceId and rejects payload-side claims with 400 (tenant binding). Canon reads (/wiki/canon, /wiki/list, /wiki/[slug]) are now tenant-scoped for members. 0.3.1: adds bench-wiki.wiki_draft tool (member-auth POST /wiki/draft) for conversation-born canon captures. 0.3.0: BREAKING — bench-wiki.wiki_ingest request shape changed from flat object to wrapped { entries: [...] } array; `body` renamed to `markdown`. Server auto-fills localHash/localMtime when omitted. New /wiki/search endpoint + restored wiki_search tool pointing at it. Callers built against 0.2.0 must update.
author: BenchAGI

bench_api_base.description: Base URL for Bench API. Override for staging.

bench_instance_id.description: Instance ID this Cowork session operates against. Leave null for personal canon.

monorepo_root.description: Absolute path to the BenchAGI monorepo root. Used by stdio MCP servers (bench-mail) to locate built CLI scripts.

bench_uid.description: Bench UID for the current user. Required by bench-mail to scope token vault reads.

encryption_key.description: Symmetric encryption key matching apps/web ENCRYPTION_KEY. Used by bench-mail's FirestoreTokenVault to decrypt OAuth tokens at rest.

google_client_id.description: Google OAuth client ID (matches apps/web GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID). Used by bench-mail for token refresh.

google_client_secret.description: Google OAuth client secret. Used by bench-mail for token refresh.

firebase_project_id.description: Firebase project ID hosting the bench-mail token vault.

google_application_credentials.description: Path to a Firebase Admin service account JSON file. Set this for stable local-dev; otherwise bench-mail falls back to gcloud Application Default Credentials.
